description: Each adapter's new() validates the backend's algorithm before storing it, rather than checking on every sign() call. This is the right tradeoff: algorithm mismatch is a configuration error detectable once at construction; paying the check cost on every signing operation would be waste. The Debug impls using finish_non_exhaustive() correctly prevent key material from appearing in logs regardless of what fields are added later. Both design choices are worth defending.
title: Algorithm validation at construction is correct design
